authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(77) — Laurel Lakes and tributaries
sources.quotedText: (77) Laurel Lakes and tributaries (Mono Co.). All year. Only artificial lures may be used. Minimum size limit: 14 inches total length. 2 trout

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(a) — General Provisions for waters listed in the special-regulations table
sources.quotedText: Unless otherwise provided, it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW
sources.quotedText: R6 Mono Laurel Lake #2 Golden Trout Fingerling Put and Grow August-September

parameters.note: Laurel Lake #2 is one of the Laurel Lakes basins (Mono Co.), which are listed as a special-regulation water in CCR §7.50(b)(77) — open all year.
Only artificial lures may be used at Laurel Lakes and tributaries.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures may be used
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures may be used.

parameters.note: 14-inch total-length minimum size limit for trout under the Laurel Lakes and tributaries special regulation.

regulations.verbatimText: Minimum size limit: 14 inches total length.

parameters.counting_rule: The table entry sets a 2-trout daily bag limit; per 14 CCR 7.50(a)(4), unless otherwise provided it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit, so the possession limit also equals 2.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 trout daily bag and possession limit

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

stockingSchedule.note: CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Laurel Lake #2 (Region 6, Mono Co.) for fingerling-size Golden Trout under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month range of August-September. Laurel Lake #2 is one of the two Laurel Lakes basins at the head of Laurel Canyon southeast of Mammoth Lakes; CDFW's numeric #1/#2 designations are not publicly mapped to a specific basin, so this record is placed in the upper (northeast) basin by convention. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.
